title: Check-In Template Assignment
description: The canonical shape of a CheckInTemplateAssignment record — a disclosed, additive entity filling the gap docs/44-PHASE-2B-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §10.2 settles ('a doctor explicitly assigns which template(s) apply to a patient') but does not itself name a persisted shape for. Batch PXP-5 (docs/44 §22), governed by docs/47-PHASE-2B-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md. An exact structural mirror of doctor-assigned-condition.schema.json's own already-approved shape. Many-per-patient, append-mostly: a row is created 'active' and may transition to 'resolved' exactly once, never reverted, never deleted. Doctor/staff-authored only: every write is a doctor/staff action, enforced server-side; the patient never assigns, edits, or resolves their own template assignment (docs/44 §4.3/§10.2). See check-in-template-assignment.md for the full disclosure of why this entity exists and how apps-script/CheckInTemplateAssignment.gs's foundationGetCurrentCheckInTemplateForPatient_() resolves an assignment into the template a patient's Check-in card actually renders.

assignment_id.description: Server-generated UUID (generateFoundationId_()), this row's own identity — never doctor/staff-supplied.

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this assignment belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Many CheckInTemplateAssignment rows may share the same patient_id (many-per-patient, append-mostly). Doctor/staff-supplied at write time — there is no patient session at assignment time.

template_id.description: One of shared/constants/template-registry.json's canonical template_id values (validated against apps-script/TemplateRegistry.gs's foundationGetRegisteredTemplateIds_() allowlist, the same convention doctor-assigned-condition.schema.json's condition_slug allowlist already established). Doctor/staff-supplied at write time — never patient-selected. Deliberately not paired with a specific version here: an assignment names which template a patient uses, not which version — foundationGetCurrentCheckInTemplateForPatient_() always resolves to that template_id's latest active Template Registry version (docs/44 §11.4), never a version pinned at assignment time.

assigned_by.description: The doctor/staff identifier who created this assignment (a free-text string, the same provenance convention doctor-assigned-condition.schema.json's assigned_by already uses — no real Doctor identity entity exists yet, docs/33 §1.4). Never patient-supplied.

assigned_at.description: Server-set (foundationNowIso_()) at creation. Never doctor/staff-supplied directly, never patient-supplied.

status.description: Every assignment starts 'active' and may transition to 'resolved' exactly once — a one-way, never-reversed, never-reverted transition, identical to doctor-assigned-condition.schema.json's own status field.

resolved_at.description: Empty-string sentinel until resolved (never null), then server-set at the moment of resolution. Mirrors doctor-assigned-condition.schema.json's own resolved_at exactly.

resolved_by.description: Empty-string sentinel until resolved (never null), then the doctor/staff identifier who resolved this assignment. Never patient-supplied.
